name: AWS WAF — GraphQL Introspection Guard
objective: Detect GraphQL introspection and schema enumeration requests on production GraphQL endpoints using AWS WAF regex matching and body inspection.
risk_mitigated: Schema discovery that accelerates abuse of hidden GraphQL operations, weak authorization paths, and overly permissive object relationships.
potential_side_effects: Developer tooling, contract testing, and internal schema validation can legitimately trigger the rule if production and staging are not clearly separated.
tuning_notes: Deploy in Count mode first. Exclude staging hostnames, trusted CI/CD source ranges, or signed internal tooling before moving to Block. Keep introspection disabled at the application layer where possible.
deployment_notes: Attach this rule ahead of generic managed rule groups so introspection attempts are visible as a first-class signal. Requires body inspection enabled for the associated WebACL resource type.
monitoring_notes: Review sampled requests and CloudWatch metrics for repeated introspection probes, suspicious user-agents, and unexpected production clients. Correlate with application-side GraphQL auditing.
